channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DeSantis backs Trump’s plans for FEMA, DOGE efforts
item.description: The governor announced his support for President Donald Trump looking to remove FEMA as a middleman after a disaster.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Member of Elon Musk’s DOGE team resigns after racist posts resurface
item.description: Marko Elez had recently been given special access by a federal judge to highly sensitive payment systems in the Department of Treasury.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: Blur of action in Washington, back to action in St. Paul
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News host Brian Bakst talks about the blistering change in Washington with U.S. Sen. Tina Smith. And the scuffle in the Minnesota House is over. We’ll discuss how lawmakers struck a deal to gavel in the session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers advance a bill to prevent transgender women from competing in women's sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A day after Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stood with President Donald Trump in Washington as he signed an executive order intended to prevent transgender athletes from competing in women's sports, he returned to Georgia where state Senate Republicans made a similar move.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: Genesee County Chris Swanson has told The Associated Press he's launching a Democratic bid to be Michigan's next governor.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Justice Department ends Biden-era task force aimed at seizing assets of Russian oligarchs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's Justice Department has disbanded a Biden-era program aimed at seizing the assets of Russian oligarchs as a means to punish Russia for its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems who lashed out at Trump for being 'threat to democracy' flip script with last-minute delay tactics
item.description: Voters overwhelmingly elected President Donald Trump to carry out his agenda despite Democrats calling him a "threat to democracy." Dems are now delaying key cabinet appointments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota GOP takes control of House after Dems' weekslong boycott, pledges to 'check' Walz's 'extreme' agenda
item.description: Republicans and Democrats in the Minnesota House have reached a "power sharing agreement" ending the Democrats' weeks-long boycott of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom changes tone on Trump from campaign rhetoric with federal wildfire recovery funds at stake
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has long been a leading adversary and target of President Donald Trump. But the governor, in need of federal help, is working with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers needed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday doubled down on his proposal for the U.S. to take control of the Gaza Strip and rebuild it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with congressional Republicans as GOP lawmakers argue over tax and spending cuts
item.description: President Donald Trump hosted an unusually long meeting with House Republicans at the White House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel defense minister tells army to prepare to relocate Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: He says the military should prepare exit options by land, sea and air so that anyone who wishes to can leave for "any country willing to accept them.
item.dc:creator: Jerome Socolovsky and Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: Rattled by politics? Try some sweet potato pie
item.description: Rose McGee, the founder of the Twin Cities nonprofit Sweet Potato Comfort Pie, hopes her comfort food will help people connect and have meaningful conversations around hard topics like race.
item.dc:creator: Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota distillery alarmed by now-delayed Canadian tariffs, retaliatory measures
item.description: The Princeton-based company exports about 5 percent of its products, including vodkas, brandies and liqueurs, to Canada. There, the company’s Butter Ripple Schnapps is used in a drink named after American actor Burt Reynolds.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Why CBS stands at the epicenter of Trump's assault on the media
item.description: CBS News appears likely to settle a lawsuit brought by President Trump in a case that legal observers say it should win. The network is caught up in Trump's broader assault on the media and an owner intent on finalizing a sale.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: FBI agents, prosecutors fear retribution from Jan. 6 rioters pardoned by Trump
item.description: Officials involved in Jan. 6 prosecutions say the Trump administration isn't protecting them from threats. "We don't think they'll care — unless and until one of us gets killed," an official told NPR.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: As 'Fork' resignation deadline arrives, a federal judge will consider a stay
item.description: The Trump administration has given more than 2 million federal employees until Thursday to decide whether to stay or go. A last-minute court hearing may throw a wrench into the administration's plans.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Department of Education: The arguments for, against killing it
item.description: Former Trump campaign surrogate Scotti Nell Hughes says the move would bring more accountability at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: US officials now say Trump only wants to displace Palestinians from Gaza temporarily
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s top diplomat and his main spokesperson on Wednesday walked back the idea that he wants the permanent relocation of Palestinians from Gaza, after American allies and even Republican lawmakers rebuffed his suggestion that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ lawmakers create new bill to combat impersonating first responders
item.description: WEST WINDSOR, N.J. (PIX11) - First responders can be some of the most trustworthy figures in our communities, but some will try to impersonate them to commit other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: "With the support of President @realDonaldTrump, the @DOGE team will aim to make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system," Musk wrote Wednesday on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: 60 Minutes publishes transcript of Harris interview amid FCC investigation
item.description: "We are posting the same transcripts and videos of our interview with Vice President Kamala Harris that we provided to the FCC," the news program wrote on its website.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Callers swamp Senate offices in Trump's first weeks, leading to busy signals and full voicemail
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Callers are getting busy signals and voicemail inboxes are full at many U.S. Senate offices as people try to reach out and voice their opinions on President Donald Trump’s Cabinet picks, executive orders and moves to dismantle various federal programs.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education a key focus for lawmakers, DeSantis in 2025 state budget
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis is urging lawmakers to consider his state budget proposal one month before they are back in Tallahassee to begin the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump unleashes a supercharged MAGA agenda and Republicans come aboard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump is testing how far Republicans are willing to go in supporting his supercharged “Make America Great Again” agenda, tearing down government agencies and taking actions fundamentally at odds with once-traditional party principles.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Stauber reintroduces bill to reverse mining ban near Boundary Waters, return leases to Twin Metals
item.description: Northern Minnesota Republican Congressman Pete Stauber has reintroduced legislation to overturn a 20-year mining ban near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. If passed, the bill would also return mineral leases to Twin Metals, the company vying to build an underground copper mine near Ely.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Freshman Democrat, physician Rep. Morrison warns against RFK Jr. nomination
item.description: Minnesota Third-District Democratic Rep. Kelly Morrison, who is one of a handful of physicians in Congress, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to express her concerns and what Kennedy’s confirmation could mean in practice.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Homeland Security's workforce might not be as big as Trump's immigration goals
item.description: Changes to hiring and retention practices grew the department by 19,000 people in four years. But former officials warn that's still not enough for Trump's ambitious policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Bill count swells to 484 as Nevada Legislature hits Day 2; Voter ID a Republican goal
item.description: Packed binders. Busy halls. Lobbyists, lawmakers and journalists roaming amongst each other. This is when the routine of the state government sets in. Already, there are 484 bills submitted this session, with many more expected.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
